must: RULE: "MUST MATCH ITEM COUNT AND ORDER EXACTLY. AUTHORITATIVE."
- REQUIREMENT: "EVERY SOURCE ITEM MUST MAP TO A DSL VERB"
- STRICT_DEPENDENCY_ORDER: "Packages MUST be ordered chronologically by dependency: 1. Infrastructure/DB (Models) -> 2. Business Logic (Backend/API) -> 3. User Interface (Views/UI)."

- WILDCARD_EXPANSION: "If any DSL entry in 'Files affected (DSL)' uses a glob/wildcard pattern (e.g. 'module/*'), the package assigned to modify those files MUST enumerate every concrete file in its '## Files to modify' section. Wildcards are disallowed in package file lists. If expansion is not determinable at plan time, list each anticipated file and mark each unknown as 'UNKNOWN: <reason>'."
RULE: "MUST BE POPULATED VIA VALIDATION RULES"

RULE: "Determine and list the exact filenames for all packages to be created (e.g., `PACKAGE_1_database.md`, `PACKAGE_2_api.md`) based on the PACKAGE_SPLIT_RULES. This establishes a canonical list of package names BEFORE they are generated."
must_not: - DOMAIN_COHESION: "STRICT ISOLATION. Database/Models MUST NOT be in the same package as UI/Frontend modifications. Each logic layer requires a separate package. Exception: Trivial configuration updates without new logic (e.g. adding a flag to config and frontend) may be bundled to avoid overhead."

optional_non_derivable.domain_specific_semantics: UNKNOWN: conversion preserves textual constraints only and does not infer domain semantics
